explanation: as in an old castle or abbey. But massive doors of this type probably to DOOR-DUNGEON.

discriminators: Age is a semantic property: no DSP separates an antique door from a modern wooden one. Expect a wood-like short ring (decay_time_seconds_60db ~0.2 s) plus loose-hinge friction, but that describes DOORWood equally.
Massive castle/abbey doors with deep low_band_energy and long reverberant tails belong to DOORDungn per the explanation.
An oven or fridge door is a damped hollow-cavity thunk with a metal latch click; it is short and heavily damped, unlike DOORMetl which rings.
Nothing in the signal says 'oven' rather than 'cabinet'. Text drives this; the signature only rules out sustained tonal or minutes-long material.

provenance.policy: mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened

discriminators: Small, light, quiet wooden panel with a magnetic-catch or latch click; shorter decay and lower level than a full DOORWood house door.
A cabinet door swings on a hinge; a drawer slides. If the dominant event is a sustained low-crest friction glide with a terminal stop, prefer DRWRWood.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: doors that don't sound like a specific material. A modern front door. Some confusion or overlap with DOORS-WOOD likely.

discriminators: The explanation says it outright: fiberglass/wood/steel composite doors 'don't sound like a specific material', so the material axis gives no leverage. Overlap with DOORWood is expected and acknowledged.
Rules out only the extremes: no long metallic ring (that is DOORMetl) and no glassy high-frequency shimmer (DOORGlas).

The archetypal friction morphology: sustained, high-centroid, low crest factor, with wildly unstable partials (large spectral_centroid_deviation_hz). A creak is not an impact and must never be confused with DOORKnck, whose crest factor is far higher and whose energy is front-loaded.
Distinguished from WOODFric / METALFric only by context: the sound is a door hinge. Morphologically identical; the explanation admits the overlap.
Rope creaks (ROPECreak) sit lower and groanier: centroid nearer 1 kHz with a strong quasi-periodic groan, versus the door hinge's 2-4 kHz squeal.

explanation: That big old classic wood and/or metal heavy dungeon

discriminators: Mass is measurable: a dungeon door is dominated by low_band_energy with a long reverberant release, plus heavy iron-hinge creak. That prunes it away from the light DOORCab / DOORPlas, but not from DOORSton or a big DOORMetl.
Massive antique doors go here rather than DOORAntq (the explanation says so). If it is light and merely old-sounding, prefer DOORAntq.

explanation: sliding store doors. See also DOORS-REVOLVING.

discriminators: The motor is the tell: a sustained, stationary, harmonic drive whine bracketed by a start clunk and an end limit-switch thud. Any manual door (DOORWood, DOORSlid, DOORGate) lacks this sustained_tonal bed entirely.
Against DOORHydr: hydraulic/pneumatic closers hiss (high spectral_flatness, low harmonicity) rather than whine (high harmonicity, low flatness). That is the separation.

explanation: metal or wood. So a garden gate
a park gate. A farm gate.

discriminators: Non-motorized by definition, so it must NOT carry a sustained motor whine — that would be DOORElec. Beyond that, a metal garden gate is acoustically a metal door: squeal, clank, latch.
Usually recorded outdoors, so a wide stereo_width and an exterior ambience bed are weak supporting evidence, not proof.

explanation: glass home front doors. Most store doors would probably fit here.

discriminators: Glass is bright: a pane rattles and shimmers with high_band_energy and a centroid well above the ~1.5 kHz of DOORWood. It is a colour cue, not a certainty — the frame and closer still dominate the low end.
This is an intact glass door being operated. A glass door being destroyed is GLASS-BREAK, not DOORGlas: that has burst morphology and a dense high-frequency debris texture tail.
Store-front glass doors on a slide rail overlap DOORSlid; a swinging glass front door overlaps DOORComp.

Small metal parts: bright, spiky, tiny clicks and jiggles with almost no low_band_energy. The absence of a big low-frequency slab impact is what separates DOORHdwr from DOORKnck and from any full-leaf door close.
A doorknob jiggle or lock pick is a burst of 4-20 micro-transients; a deadbolt is 1-2. Both stay bright and quiet.
Physically identical to WNDWHdwr — only the fixture differs, so text must break the tie.

explanation: something not quite sci-fi but more advanced

discriminators: A designed layer stack: pneumatic seal hiss (sustained_noise) plus a servo whine (sustained_tonal) plus a heavy vault thunk. The presence of BOTH a noise bed and a tonal bed inside one file is the cue, and it separates DOORTech from plain DOORMetl.
Overlaps DOORHydr (the un-designed real-world version of the same hiss) and SCIFI-DOOR (fully synthetic, typically brighter and more processed).

explanation: lots of office and store doors are of this type. Also automatic doors if hydraulic or pneumatic.

discriminators: Air is noise: a self-closer or pneumatic ram is a broadband hiss (high spectral_flatness, near-zero harmonicity) that ends in a latch thunk. The hiss bed is the whole diagnosis.
Against DOORElec: an electric motor is HARMONIC and pitched (harmonicity high, flatness low). Same duration, opposite spectrum. This is the cleanest split in the DOORS category after CREAK vs KNOCK.

provenance.policy: mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened

mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened

discriminators: A knock is a TRAIN: 2-4 (sometimes more) mid-band impulses at roughly 2-4 per second with high onset_periodicity. transient_count > 1 is a hard gate — a single isolated slab impact is not a knock.

Against a slam or a heavy door close: a slam is ONE loud low-frequency-heavy impact with a room tail (low_band_energy high, transient_count = 1). A knock is repeated, mid-band, and far quieter in absolute terms. transient_count and onset_rate_per_second separate them cleanly.
Against DOORCreak: opposite morphology. Knock is front-loaded and spiky (crest_factor ~13, sustain_ratio < 0.1); a creak is sustained and low-crest.
Against WNDWKnck: same gesture, brighter surface. Knuckles on glass push the centroid above ~2.5 kHz with a ringing pane; knuckles on a wooden door sit near 1.2 kHz and die in ~0.1 s.

explanation: like a vault door or heavy door. PRISON doors have their own category
as they are quite specific sounding.

discriminators: Metal RINGS. decay_time_seconds_60db near 1 s with high inharmonicity and a rich partial_count is the material-impedance signature, and it is the single boundary against DOORWood, whose ring dies in roughly 0.1 s.
The explanation carves out prison doors as their own sound: if the clang is huge, low, sliding and reverberant, prefer DOORPrisn.

explanation: Door sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: The abstention target for DOORS. By definition it has no distinguishing morphology: it is whatever door sound the other 21 subcategories reject.
Deliberately un-gated so it can always receive an abstained file.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: Doors made out of plastic. Not very common but maybe a door to a child's play house.

discriminators: Plastic is DEAD and light: the shortest ring of any door material (decay_time_seconds_60db well under 0.1 s), a hollow mid-high rattle, and very little low_band_energy. That is a real contrast against DOORWood (thud with body) and DOORMetl (long ring).
Still semantic_only in practice: a thin plastic door and a thin hollow-core wooden door converge, and the category is rare enough that text must carry it.

explanation: Big metal jail and prison doors. Sliding prison doors

discriminators: Mass plus a hard reverberant room: the longest metal ring in the category, an enormous clang, and a slide/rumble before the bolt. Both the decay and the room tail run longer than a generic DOORMetl.

Prison doors are called out in the DOORS-METAL explanation as 'quite specific sounding', which is exactly the cue to trust here — but only material and size are measurable, not incarceration, so a heavy industrial fire door will land here too.

explanation: Classic revolving doors used as entries to hotels

discriminators: A revolving door never stops and never slams: a long, low-crest, near-stationary rubber-seal swish and glass-panel rumble with almost no transients. The absence of impacts is the diagnosis.
Against DOORSlid: sliding is a one-way glide that ENDS in a stop thud; revolving is continuous and cyclic, and usually carries a lobby ambience bed.

explanation: sliding shower doors. Electric sliding doors to DOORS-ELECTRIC.

provenance.policy: mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened
mean replaced by the measured median; deviation widened only, never tightened

discriminators: A roller glide: sustained low-crest friction across the whole file with a terminal impact when the leaf hits the jamb. Energy sits mid-file (envelope_temporal_centroid ~0.5), unlike any hinged door, which is front-loaded.
The explanation is explicit: manual only. If a sustained motor whine is present it is DOORElec, and if a hiss bed is present it is DOORHydr.
Against DRWRWood/DRWRMetl: a drawer is the same friction+stop morphology at a fraction of the duration and level. Sliding doors run seconds; drawers run well under one.

Stone does not ring: it is a sustained, dark, gritty low-frequency grind with a steep negative spectral tilt and near-zero harmonicity. The very low spectral_centroid_hz plus long sustain is what separates it from every other door in the category.
Mostly a designed/fantasy sound (Gates of Moria), so it is usually layered with rumble and debris. That reinforces the low-band prior rather than weakening it.

explanation: like old-west saloon or church pulpit doors. Doors on swinging springs.

discriminators: A saloon door flaps: a decaying train of wood-on-wood slaps with a spring squeak between them. Multiple onsets whose amplitude decays over seconds is the signature, and it is genuinely measurable.
Against DOORKnck: a knock is a short deliberate burst of even-strength raps on a stationary door; a swing is a longer, decaying, less periodic flutter with hinge friction interleaved.
Against DOORWood: a single close is one transient; a swing is five or more.

Wood is DAMPED. A wooden door closing is a mid-band thud whose ring collapses in roughly 0.1-0.2 s (decay_time_seconds_60db), with a broad low-mid body and modest inharmonicity. State the boundary from this side: METAL doors ring for ~1 s or more with high inharmonicity and a dense partial_count; wood does not ring at all by comparison.
The catch-all door material per the explanation, so this is deliberately a generous prior rather than a tight one.
Against DOORCab: same material, smaller and quieter, with a magnetic catch.
